channel.title: Iot on The Mize Collection
channel.description: Recent content in Iot on The Mize Collection

item.title: Software Defined Radio
item.description:
When I found out there was an affordable device capable of listening to a wide range of frequencies (10 Hz – 2 GHz from my tests) and giving me a glimpse into the Ham Radio world, which decades ago would have likely cost me thousands and required huge racks of hardware, I knew I had to get my hands on one.

I got the RTL-SDR V3, and my main goal was to try to capture signals from old weather satellites that transmit live images of Earth. After browsing the topic, I knew NOAA (National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration) should be my first target, since all you need is a dipole antenna, an SDR (Software Defined Radio) device, and a computer to get started.
